frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: history biases enter as drift bias (parietal) and starting-point bias (motor)

frameworks.name: choice history bias
frameworks.one_line: idiosyncratic tendency to repeat or alternate previous perceptual choices

frameworks.name: persistent neural activity
frameworks.one_line: sustained band-limited cortical activity carries previous-trial information across trials

core_question: Which cortical circuits produce idiosyncratic choice history biases in perceptual decisions, and how?
key_assumptions: history biases can be decomposed into accumulation parameters (drift bias vs starting-point bias)
MEG band-limited power indexes circuit-level persistent activity
sensory stimuli are presented in random sequence so biases are endogenous
manipulations: behavioral modeling: drift bias vs starting-point bias parameterization
key_findings: parietal gamma-band activity sustainedly tracks previous choice
motor beta-band activity inversely reflects previous motor action
parietal signal biases drift / evidence accumulation toward repetition; motor signal biases starting point toward alternation
parietal (not motor) signal mediates impact of previous on current choice and explains individual differences in repetition
interpretations: distinct parietal vs motor persistent signals implement dissociable accumulation biases
posterior parietal cortex is the locus of individual-level choice repetition bias
